body:
In b7514493a0c4a4389a5921f15b0b429dae5c75e7 the default for shortmess was changed to include `F`:

>	  F	don't give the file info when editing a file, like `:silent`
>		was used for the command

However, this suppresses also output from `:echom` during ftplugin handling.

I'm using `echohl + echom` in jedi-vim to display errors when loading Jedi (Python) fails, but `shortmess+=F` suppresses this.

I think jedi-vim can be improved in this regard anyway, but changing this default has some rather serious impact I would say - given that it behaves as expected/documented.
I could also imagine that Vim/Neovim could be fixed here to not suppress output from echo/echom, but only suppress the file info really.
(Vim behaves the same when `shortmess+=F` is used)

comment_data.body:
Yes.
I just reported it here since the changed defaults triggered it.
(and I think the defaults should not be changed until this is fixed)
Should ftplugins really use `:echom` for anything important? (Or for anything at all...)

> Should ftplugins really use :echom for anything important? (Or for anything at all...)

What would be the alternative to communicate with the user?
ftplugins generally should not be "communicating". They are only for setting filetype-specific settings and other initialization.

And what should happen in case of errors?
Use `:echoerr` in general, and report it in another way otherwise (like done for jedi-vim now in https://github.com/blueyed/jedi-vim/commit/cfe1668d72152667e7da43ff0e2d38903ba1e6dc)?
I think in general using `:silent` should be avoided, and `shortmess+=F` basically does this (according to the help at least).

> And what should happen in case of errors?

Why would a ftplugin have errors? They are supposed to be simple.

> :silent should be avoided, and shortmess+=F basically does this 

Good point, `:silent edit ...` has a more drastic effect that I had thought. But, I'm still not sure it's so bad.

> Why would a ftplugin have errors? They are supposed to be simple.

In the case of jedi-vim it might load Jedi (via Python), and that can cause various errors.
The errors are handled and displayed using echohl/echom.
I think I suggested we should change that once. I don't remember if there was a reason for it. I'm in favor of changing it.

> The errors are handled and displayed using echohl/echom.

Is that because `echoerr` doesn't show up in `:messages`?

Edit: No, because they do... So why not use `:echoerr`?
So the jedi error currently appears in messages, right? Seems fine.

Not with `shortmess+=F` anymore.
https://github.com/davidhalter/jedi-vim/pull/840/files is a workaround for jedi-vim (and good in general), but this changing of the default might affect others.

This new default setting has been released by now, and jedi-vim was fixed to not use echom anymore for reporting errors.
Closing.

JFI: `:unsilent echom` can be used.
This is useful for multiline output, since you can only have one `:echoerr`.

Another use case: DidYouMean, which uses `inputlist()` via `BufNewFile` autocommand.
The fix here is also to use `nosilent` (https://github.com/EinfachToll/DidYouMean/pull/12).

milestone.description: Low priority items. We plan to work on this but don't have a target date.
milestone.title: backlog

title: shortmess+=F suppresses output from :echom in ftplugin
